product_brief: Managed service to build, run, and scale production-ready Kubernetes applications across any environment.
pqc_capability_description: PQC support is implemented in specific AWS services integrated with EKS environments, including AWS Transfer Family (SFTP) and TLS 1.3 via AWS SDK for Java v2. Algorithms include ML-KEM (hybridized with SecP256r1/X25519). Status is active for SFTP security policies and TLS client configuration; not explicitly stated as a native kernel-level feature of the EKS control plane itself.
crypto_primitives: ML-KEM (ML-KEM-768, ML-KEM-1024), SecP256r1, X25519, NIST P-256, NIST P-384, SHA-256, SHA-384
key_management_model: AWS Key Management Service (KMS) and CloudHSM integration; customer responsible for client-side encryption and OS configuration.
architecture_type: Hybrid cloud infrastructure with managed control plane; PQC implemented via SDK libraries (AWS-LC) and service-level security policies (Transfer Family).
latest_version: AWS SDK for Java v2.0; AWS-LC-FIPS-3.0
release_date: 2025-03-24 (Presentation date); FIPS 203 IPD August 24, 2023; ML-KEM TLS 1.3 Policies January 2025
fips_validated: Yes (AWS-LC-FIPS-3.0 pending CMVP; FIPS 203 IPD mentioned)
primary_platforms: Amazon EKS, AWS Cloud, EC2, Linux/Windows (implied by Kubernetes/OpenSSH context)
target_industries: Financial services, healthcare, media & entertainment, software, AI/ML workloads
regulatory_status: NIST SP800-208, CNSA 2.0, FIPS 203, FIPS 204, FIPS 205 compliance timelines referenced
pqc_roadmap_details: Migration strategy includes inventorying use cases, addressing long-term confidentiality, and upgrading hard-to-migrate roots of trust. Timeline targets 2025 for ML-KEM TLS/SFTP adoption, with full CNSA 2.0 (ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87) by 2030-2035.
signature_schemes: ML-DSA (planned per NIST timeline), LMS/XMSS (CNSA 2.0); currently using hybrid ECDSA/Ed25519 with ML-KEM for key exchange.
